summary:
Melanotic neuroectodermal tumor of infancy most commonly occurs in the maxilla, which accounts for approximately 62% of cases, significantly exceeding other sites such as the skull and mandible. The consistent prevalence across multiple studies solidifies the maxilla as the primary anatomic location involved. \boxed{A}

- Eliminate repetition and any extraneous phrasing ("solidifies," "consistent," etc.).

Corrected Final Summary:
Melanotic neuroectodermal tumors of infancy primarily affect the maxilla, observed in up to 62% of cases. The maxilla is thus recognized as the most frequent site based on established pathological distributions. \boxed{A}
